texts.full_text: ne 2001 (43) May 2001 (56) April 2001 (71) March 2001 (77) February 2001 (69) January 2001 (106) The NYPD’s Record On Chokeholds German Lopez voxplains a new set of findings: The report, by the NYPD inspector general, looked at 10 cases involving chokeholds between 2009 and 2014. The Civilian Complaint Review Board recommended the most serious penalty in nine of 10 cases, but the NYPD reduced the punishment to lesser penalties — or none at all — in the cases that have been carried out to completion. The NYPD’s guidelines explicitly ban the use of chokeholds no matter the circumstance. But the inspector general found police officers, in a practice called “particularly alarming” by the report, sometimes used chokeholds “as a first act of physical force in response to verbal resistance.” Friedersdorf is troubled: Consider one of those incidents: In a Nov. 19, 2008, incident, a 15-year-old detained on robbery charges alleged he was choked by a sergeant while handcuffed to a rail inside a Bronx precinct house. CCRB substantiated the allegation based on another teenage witness in the station and the sergeant’s account. Then-Commissioner Ray Kelly decided to impose the following punishment: no punishment at all. New York City is policed on the theory that if small transgressions against law-and-order go unpunished, the ensuing disorder will result in a city where more serious crimes l

texts.full_text: was awarded the Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers in 2016 from Barack Obama. He is a Fellow of the American Physical Society. He is a Nigerian-American. Early life and education Akinwande was born in Washington, DC and moved to Nigeria in his early years. He grew up in Ikeja with his parents. His father was the financial controller of Guardian News and his mother worked at the Ministry of Education. Akinwande attended St. Gregory's College, a Catholic boarding school in Jos, Plateau State. He graduated from the University of Nigeria, Nsukka (UNN) with a degree in electrical engineering in 1997 and then went on to obtain his Master's degree from the University of Texas at Austin in 2001. Akinwande completed his Ph.D. from the University of Texas at Austin in 2005. Career Akinwande began his career as a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Texas at Austin in 2005. He joined the University of Texas at Austin as an assistant professor in 2008 and was promoted to associate professor in 2012. In 2014, he was promoted to full professor. Akinwande's research focuses on developing novel materials for energy storage, flexible electronics, and sensors. His work has led to significant advancements in the field of nanotechnology and has been published in numerous scientific journals. He is the recipient of several awards and honors, including the National Science Foundation Career Award, the Alfred P. Sloan Research Fellowship, and the Materials Research Society Outstanding Young Investigator Award. Ak

We evaluated 15 patients 3-39 years of age withnonpalpable testeswho had undergone computed tomographic (CT) venography, whichwas performed using a helical CT scanner and reconstructions at both the coronal and axial planes to demonstrate the pampiniform plexus. The results showed that in all patients with an intra-abdominalor inguinoscrotaltestis, visualization of blood flow within the vas deferens on color Doppler ultrasound was areliable indicator for proceeding with venography because it suggested either ectopic insertion into the opposite internal iliac artery andvein (contralateral to side beingevaluated). In cases where there is no apparentconnection between these vessels, or if only one testis can be identified by physical examination alone without any signsof vascular connections via color Doppler ultrasound imagingwhich indicates absence from inguinoscrotal position due its locationoutside normal range forsuch positioning - then further diagnostic procedures may need consideration including exploratory laparotomy. The accuracy and reliability provided through this method has been proven time again; however, caution should still remain when interpreting results as they are not always definitive indicators of a nonpalpable testis' presence or absence due to potential technicallimitations inherent within the imaging modality itself (suchis true for any diagnostic tool). texts.full_text: antipyrine. A comparative study in four animal species: cattle, goat, rat and rabbit. 1. The plasma disposition of antipyrine, and its urinary metabolite pattern, were studied in both sexes of four animal species: rat, dwarf goat, rabbit and cattle. 2. No sex differences in plasma elimination of antipyrine were found in rabbit and goat; however, in rat and cattle the effect of sex was marked. As expected, male rat showed a higher plasma clearance value than female. In contrast bulls showed a significantly lower clearance value than cows. 3. Metabolite patterns varied widely from one species to another. The major urinary metabolite in rabbit and the two ruminant species was 4-hydroxy-antipyrine (OHAntipyrene), while acetylated antipurines were predominant in rats, reflecting differences not only between sexes but also across different animal classes. 1. Species-Specific Metabolism: The study highlights the diversity of metabolic pathways for drugs like antipirin among species and even within specific groups such as ruminants (cattle vs goat). This suggests that extrapolating drug metabolism data from one species to another can be misleading without considering these inherent differences. 2. Sex-Related Differences in Metabolism: The findings regarding sex-related variations, particularly the higher clearance rate of antipyrine observed in male rats compared with females and lower rates seen in bulls as opposed to cows underscore how physiological factors such as hormonal influences might affect drug metabolism differently across species sexes. 3. Implications for Veterinary Medicine: - These differences are crucial when determining appropriate dosages, frequencies, or even the choice of drugs used within veterinary practices tailored specifically towards certain animal types and their respective sex. 4. Research Impacts: This comparative study not only aids in understanding interspecies variability but also encourages further research into genetic factors that might contribute to these observed patterns. 5. Considerations for Human Medicine: - While this particular investigation focuses on animals, the principles derived can be informative when considering how humans metabolize drugs differently based upon sex and possibly other demographic or biological variables. Overall, such studies are essential in pharmacokinetics research as they help develop more effective therapeutic strategies by acknowledging both species-specific c

texts.full_text: aim of this systematic review by the APSA Outcomes and Evidence Based Practice Committee was to evaluate the evidence regarding treatment of organ injuries in children. A comprehensive search strategy was crafted and the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analysis (PRISMA) guidelines were utilized to identify, review, and report salient articles. Four principal questions were examined based upon the previously published consensus APSA guidelines regarding length of stay (LOS), activity level, radiologic procedures, and follow-up imaging. We developed a fluorescent protein construct by genetically fusing green fluorescent protein (GFP) to aspartate dehydrogenase from Thermotoga maritima. The fusion protein was cloned, heterologously expressed in Escherichia coli cells, and purified by Ni-chelate affinity chromatography. It was then introduced into a measurement cuvette to monitor its fluorescence signal. Aspartate dehydrogenase functioned as the biorecognition element, and aspartate-induced conformational change was converted to a fluorescence signal through the attached GFP, allowing for real-time monitoring of aspartate concentrations. Personal loans are a good option to get the money you need, but only if you have a good credit score, otherwise the interest rates can be even higher than the highest interest rate credit card. If you do opt to get a personal loan with bad credit, it's important to shop around and compare offers from multiple lenders in Calhoun-Riceville, Tennessee. texts.full_text: day laborer or at warehouses and shipping companies to support his family. "It was all survival," he said. Mr. Grayson's journey began to change when he heard about the Los Angeles Black Worker Center from an acquaintance. The center, founded in 2013 by a coalition of labor unions and community organizations, aims to connect African Americans with well-paying jobs that offer benefits and opportunities for advancement. He attended one of their workshops on apprenticeship programs offered through local unions. That's where he learned about the carpenters’ union, which seemed like a viable path out of his current circumstances. The center provided him with guidance throughout the application process, helped him prepare for interviews and tests, and even assisted in securing childcare so he could attend training sessions. "It was tough at first," Mr. Grayson recalled, "But knowing that there were people rooting for me made all the difference." He spent months working as an apprentice carpenter before finally being accepted into the union full-time. His new job provides him with a stable income and benefits, allowing him to save money for his family's future. "I never thought I could have this kind of opportunity," Mr. Grayson said, looking back on his journey from day laborer to skilled carpenter. texts.full_text: to say, several files were not copied since those files were busy. My hope was to reboot and rerun spuninst.bat from c: . The path I wrote down where the spuninst.bat file is located is C:winnt$NTServicePackUninstall$Spuninst. Unfortunately, I don't know how to get to $NTServicePackUninstall$. What exactly do the $ signs mean? I hope I'm not asking for too much of an education here. I can use my recover disk, but I hoped this would do it for me. Windows 2000 no longer loads. It'I understand that you're trying to navigate through your system files and are encountering some unfamiliar symbols like the dollar sign ($). Let's break down what these signs mean in this context: 1) Dollar Sign ( $ ): In many programming languages, including those used by Windows operating systems for internal references or variables within file paths especially related with environment variable expansion. However when you see them outside of an expanded path like C:\$MyFolder$, it's usually because the command prompt is trying to represent something dynamic about your system. 2) Backslash ( \ ): It denotes a directory change in DOS/Windows batch files, similar how forward slashes (/), are used for changing directories on Unix-like operating systems.
